chapter_goal: Make iterative MVG estimation inspectable through reprojection residuals, damping, robust influence, and sparse bundle-adjustment algebra.

libraries.matplotlib: durable contours, image residual arrows, attenuation plots, and sparsity diagrams
libraries.numpy: residuals, analytic Jacobians, dense 2x2 optimizer steps
libraries.scipy.sparse: bundle-adjustment block Jacobian and normal equations

visual_sequence.concept: Gauss-Newton and Levenberg-Marquardt on image reprojection error
visual_sequence.inspection_target: compare image residual arrows with the path through yaw/translation parameter space
visual_sequence.validation: LM final objective is lower than the starting objective and the analytic Jacobian matches finite differences

visual_sequence.concept: local linearization and damping as a trust-region mechanism
visual_sequence.inspection_target: compare exact contours, quadratic model contours, and damped step lengths from the same start
visual_sequence.validation: damped step norms decrease as lambda increases and predicted reductions stay positive

visual_sequence.inspection_target: read objective, lambda, trust ratio, and pixel residual traces together
visual_sequence.validation: accepted objective values are monotone non-increasing

visual_sequence.concept: robust attenuation and leverage for contaminated image measurements
visual_sequence.inspection_target: outlier residuals remain visible while their attenuation and leverage are reported
visual_sequence.validation: Cauchy attenuation gives outliers lower weight and improves clean-data residuals over ordinary LS

visual_sequence.concept: bundle-adjustment block sparsity and Schur elimination
visual_sequence.inspection_target: trace camera-point visibility into the Jacobian, normal matrix, and camera Schur complement
visual_sequence.validation: camera and point steps from Schur elimination agree with the dense solve
